message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C1",
      "title": "Fundamental Concepts and Measurement",
      "description": "Ability to understand the nature of matter, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations, and handle measurement uncertainties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the importance of Chemistry in various disciplines and career paths.",
        "Distinguish between macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic representations of chemical phenomena.",
        "Apply Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain atomic and molecular masses.",
        "Perform stoichiometric calculations involving mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, and limiting reagents.",
        "Identify sources of uncertainty in measurement and apply rules for significant figures."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry",
      "chapter_name": "Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C2",
      "title": "Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics",
      "description": "Understanding the evolution from classical models to quantum mechanical descriptions, including electronic configurations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Trace the historical development of atomic models leading to the Bohr model.",
        "Explain the postulates of the Bohr model for hydrogen atoms and its limitations.",
        "Describe the transition towards the Quantum Mechanical Model of the atom.",
        "Apply quantum numbers to describe electronic configurations."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Structure of Atom",
      "chapter_name": "Structure of Atom"
    },
    {
      "code": "C3",
      "title": "Periodic Classification and Trends",
      "description": "Ability to classify elements based on electronic configuration, understand the modern periodic law, and predict trends in properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Justify the need for classification of elements and explain the genesis of periodic tables.",
        "Apply the Modern Periodic Law to determine element positions and nomenclature (Z > 100).",
        "Correlate electronic configuration with s, p, d, f block classifications.",
        "Analyze periodic trends in atomic/ionic radii, ionization enthalpy, electron gain enthalpy, electronegativity, and valency."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties",
      "chapter_name": "Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties"
    },
    {
      "code": "C4",
      "title": "Chemical Bonding Theories",
      "description": "Mastery of various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) to predict molecular geometry and stability.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate between ionic, covalent, and coordinate bonds using Kossel-Lewis approach.",
        "Predict molecular shapes using VSEPR theory based on electron pair repulsion.",
        "Explain hybridization concepts (sp, sp2, sp3) and their relation to bond parameters.",
        "Apply Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory to explain bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure",
      "chapter_name": "Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure"
    },
    {
      "code": "C5",
      "title": "Thermodynamic Principles and Spontaneity",
      "description": "Ability to analyze energy changes in chemical processes, calculate enthalpy variations, and determine reaction spontaneity.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define thermodynamic terms (system, surroundings, state functions) and classify systems.",
        "Calculate internal energy change (∆U) and enthalpy change (∆H) using calorimetry data.",
        "Apply Hess's Law to determine reaction enthalpies for various types of reactions.",
        "Relate Gibbs Energy Change (∆G) to spontaneity and equilibrium conditions."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Thermodynamics",
      "chapter_name": "Thermodynamics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C6",
      "title": "Chemical Equilibrium Dynamics",
      "description": "Understanding dynamic equilibria in physical and chemical processes, including ionic equilibrium and buffer systems.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria and apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium.",
        "Calculate equilibrium constants (K) and relate them to reaction quotient (Q) and Gibbs Energy (G).",
        "Predict factors affecting equilibrium shifts according to Le Chatelier's principle.",
        "Analyze ionic equilibrium in solutions, including acid-base ionization, buffer action, and solubility product."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Equilibrium",
      "chapter_name": "Equilibrium"
    },
    {
      "code": "C7",
      "title": "Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes",
      "description": "Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, balance redox equations, and understand electrode reactions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define oxidation and reduction in terms of electron transfer and classical ideas.",
        "Assign oxidation numbers using established rules to identify oxidizing and reducing agents.",
        "Balance complex redox reactions using the ion-electron method or oxidation number method.",
        "Explain the role of redox reactions in electrode processes."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Redox Reactions",
      "chapter_name": "Redox Reactions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C8",
      "title": "Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms",
      "description": "Competence in nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, purification techniques, and quantitative analysis of organic compounds.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply IUPAC rules for naming organic compounds and identifying structural representations.",
        "Differentiate between various types of isomers (structural, stereoisomers).",
        "Analyze fundamental concepts in organic reaction mechanisms including electron movement and intermediates.",
        "Select appropriate methods for purification and qualitative/quantitative analysis of organic substances."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques",
      "chapter_name": "Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques"
    },
    {
      "code": "C9",
      "title": "Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity",
      "description": "Understanding the structure, properties, and reactions of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify hydrocarbons based on saturation and structural features (aliphatic vs aromatic).",
        "Predict physical properties and chemical reactivity patterns for saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons.",
        "Explain the structure of benzene and its unique stability compared to other cyclic compounds.",
        "Assess environmental impacts such as carcinogenicity and toxicity associated with specific hydrocarbon classes."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Hydrocarbons",
      "chapter_name": "Hydrocarbons"
    },
    {
      "code": "C10",
      "title": "Solutions and Colligative Properties",
      "description": "Ability to express solution concentrations, understand vapor pressure phenomena, and apply colligative properties for molar mass determination.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate various expressions of concentration (molarity, molality, mole fraction) in solutions.",
        "Explain Raoult's Law and deviations from ideal behavior in liquid-liquid solutions.",
        "Apply colligative property laws to determine molar masses of solutes.",
        "Distinguish between normal and abnormal molecular masses based on association/dissociation."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Solutions",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 1: Solutions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C11",
      "title": "Electrochemistry Applications",
      "description": "Understanding electrochemical cells, Nernst equation applications, conductance phenomena, and corrosion mechanisms.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate between galvanic and electrolytic cells and explain their working principles.",
        "Apply the Nernst Equation to calculate cell potential under non-standard conditions.",
        "Analyze factors affecting conductivity in electrolytic solutions (molar conductance, specific conductance).",
        "Explain practical applications including batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion prevention."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electrochemistry",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 2: Electrochemistry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12",
      "title": "Chemical Kinetics Analysis",
      "description": "Ability to determine reaction rates, derive integrated rate laws, and analyze factors influencing reaction speed.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define and calculate the average and instantaneous rates of chemical reactions.",
        "Derive and apply zero-order and first-order integrated rate equations.",
        "Analyze temperature dependence of reaction rates using Arrhenius equation concepts.",
        "Explain collision theory and activation energy requirements for effective collisions."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Chemical Kinetics",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 3: Chemical Kinetics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C13",
      "title": "d-Block and f-Block Chemistry",
      "description": "Understanding the general properties, electronic configurations, and chemical behavior of transition elements and inner transition metals.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the position of d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table based on configuration.",
        "Analyze trends in physical and chemical properties (oxidation states, magnetic properties) across periods/groups.",
        "Describe important compounds and applications of Transition Elements, Lanthanoids, and Actinoids."
      ],
      "unit_name": "d and f Block Elements",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 4: d and f Block Elements"
    },
    {
      "code": "C14",
      "title": "Coordination Chemistry Principles",
      "description": "Mastery of Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories (Valence Bond & MO), and applications of coordination compounds.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply IUPAC rules for naming complex coordination compounds.",
        "Identify different types of isomerism in coordination complexes (structural and stereoisomers).",
        "Explain bonding characteristics using Valence Bond Theory, Crystal Field Theory concepts, and Metal Carbonyls."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Coordination Compounds",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 5: Coordination Compounds"
    },
    {
      "code": "C15",
      "title": "Organic Functional Group Chemistry (Haloalkanes/Ethers)",
      "description": "Understanding nomenclature, preparation methods, physical properties, and substitution/elimination reactions of halo compounds and ethers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify haloalkanes and haloarenes based on structure and hybridization."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Haloalkanes and Haloarenes",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 6: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes"
    },
    {
      "code": "C16",
      "title": "Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers Reactivity",
      "description": "Ability to distinguish between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure and predict their chemical reactions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate the acidic nature of phenol compared to alcohol."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 7: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers"
    },
    {
      "code": "C17",
      "title": "Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry",
      "description": "Understanding the chemistry of aldehydes and ketones including preparation methods, physical properties, and nucleophilic addition reactions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between aldehydes and ketones based on functional group structure."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids"
    },
    {
      "code": "C18",
      "title": "Amines Chemistry",
      "description": "Knowledge of amine classification, nomenclature, basicity trends, diazonium salts chemistry, and their synthetic utility.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify amines as primary, secondary, or tertiary based on structure."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Amines",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 9: Amines"
    },
    {
      "code": "C19",
      "title": "Biomolecules and Biochemistry Basics",
      "description": "Understanding the structure, classification, and biological functions of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify biomolecules into monosaccharides, disaccharides, polysaccharides."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Biomolecules",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 10: Biomolecules"
    },
    {
      "code": "C20",
      "title": "Practical Laboratory Skills and Safety",
      "description": "Competence in performing standard laboratory techniques, handling apparatus safely, conducting volumetric analysis, salt analysis, and executing investigatory projects.",
      "competencies": [
        "Demonstrate proficiency in basic glassware manipulation (cutting, bending, boring).",
        "Perform quantitative estimations using titration methods with high accuracy."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Practical Syllabus",
      "chapter_name": "PRACTICAL SYLLABUS"
    }
  ]
}
